history.title: "Cambridge IELTS 16 Academic Reading Test 3 — Roman shipbuilding / Climate change artefacts / Plant thermometer"

errors.detail: 原文只说 mortise and tenon 是一种技术，没提是否需要 skilled craftsmen。脑补信息导致把 NOT GIVEN 选成了 TRUE。
题干说 building hull before frame，原文说 building frame first then hull，直接矛盾。
题目问什么被用来确保同步，原文说 music，用户填了 oarsmen（人不是工具）。
题目问 agricultural goods 的例子，原文 grain from Egypt's Nile valley，用户填了地点 Nile 而非农产品 grain。
D段明确说 hunters discarded broken bows，对应 weapons left behind。
C段引用说 fieldwork is hard work，对应 physical difficulties。
F段说 colder climate → crop failures → less food，对应 less food available。
G段说 hides 和 antlers 的需求，对应 items that would have been traded。
B段说 race the clock，对应 pressure to work quickly。
原文用词是 microorganisms，用户填了语义合理但非原文的 decay。Gap Fill 必须用原文原词。
空格是主语（什么聚集在那里），原文说 reindeer congregated，用户填了地点 patches。
双选题选了 B，漏了另一个正确选项 C（某些时期文物很少）。
原文说 tougher crops（抗逆作物），没提 require less water（需要更少水），属于 NOT GIVEN。
原文说 shade 下植物长得更快（to find sunlight），题干说阳光下更快，直接矛盾 FALSE。
白天变化 <1秒（rapid），夜晚逐渐变化（gradual），题干说 same speed 与原文矛盾。
H段说 outstanding collaborators 能 help transfer knowledge into the field，对应 specialists who can make use of findings。E段讲光暗竞争机制，无关。
原文说 cold spring → colder summer → rain-soaked。NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS。
原文说 using a mustard plant called Arabidopsis，空格前是 species of。

answers.text: The Romans' shipbuilding skills were passed on to the Greeks and the Egyptians.
Skilled craftsmen were needed for the mortise and tenon method of fixing planks.
The later practice used by Mediterranean shipbuilders involved building the hull before the frame.
The Romans called the Mediterranean Sea Mare Nostrum because they dominated its use.
Most rowers on ships were people from the Roman army.
Warships were designed so that they were ____ and moved quickly.
A battering ram made of ____ was included in the design.
Warships, such as the 'trireme', had rowers on three different ____.
Unlike warships, merchant ships had a broad ____ that lay far below the surface.
They had both square and ____ sails.
On merchant ships and warships, ____ was used to ensure rowers moved their oars in and out of the water at the same time.
Quantities of agricultural goods such as ____ were transported by merchant ships to two main ports in Italy.
The ships were pulled to the shore by ____.
an explanation for weapons being left behind in the mountains
a reference to the physical difficulties involved in an archaeological expedition
an explanation of why less food may have been available
a reference to the possibility of future archaeological discoveries
examples of items that would have been traded
a reference to the pressure archaeologists are under to work quickly
Organic materials such as animal skins and textiles have little protection against ____.
____ gathered there in the summer months to avoid being attacked.
...to avoid being attacked by ____ on lower ground.
Hunters went into the mountains even during periods of extreme cold.
The number of artefacts from certain time periods was relatively low.
Hunters at this time benefited from an increased demand for goods.
Vikings did not rely on ships alone to transport goods.
The Cambridge scientists' discovery caused surprise among other scientists.
The target for agricultural production by 2050 could be missed.
Wheat and rice suffer from a rise in temperatures.
It may be possible to develop crops that require less water.
Plants grow faster in sunlight than in shade.
Phytochromes change their state at the same speed day and night.
mention of specialists who can make use of the research findings
a reference to a potential benefit of the research findings
scientific support for a traditional saying
a reference to people traditionally making plans based on plant behaviour
a reference to where the research has been reported
Daffodils are likely to flower early in response to ____.
If ash trees come into leaf before oak trees, the weather in ____ will probably be wet.
The research was carried out using a particular species of ____.

synonyms.to: learned to build ships from the people they conquered
building the frame first and then the hull
few or no signs of activity
very fast, less than a second

synonyms.from: specialists who can make use of
synonyms.to: outstanding collaborators... transfer into the field

history.title: "Cambridge IELTS 19 Academic Reading Test 1 — Passage 2: The pirates of the ancient Mediterranean"

errors.detail: 原文 D 段 king of Alashiya rejected claims，题干 denial of involvement。rejected = denial 的同义替换没识别。
G 段整段讲 Pompey 分13区→舰队清扫→安置务农的完整操作流程。
C 段明确说 governments resort to pirates during wartime，题干 circumstances = during wartime。
A 段首句 image springs to most people's minds。首段首句忽略。
G 段末 offered land far from the sea。注意同段可重复使用。
B 段 ships could not cross open water → restricted to coastal routes。
双选题只选了 B 漏了 D。D 证据: inhabitants relied heavily on marine resources。
双选题只选了 E 漏了 C。C 证据: high-ranking members engaged in piracy。
原文 demanding punishment。被中间 in the Senate 干扰，空格填被呼吁的对象。

answers.text: a reference to a denial of involvement in piracy
details of how a campaign to eradicate piracy was carried out
circumstances in which states would make use of pirates
how people today commonly view pirates
how some people were encouraged not to return to piracy
need for sailing vessels to stay close to land
They escaped capture because they knew the local area well.
They depended more on the sea than on farming for survival.
Important officials were known to take part in piracy.
A favourable view of piracy is evident in ancient Greek texts.
attacks on vessels transporting ____ to Rome
resulted in calls for ____ for the pirates responsible
pirates demanding a ____

synonyms.to: image springs to most people's minds
offered land far from the sea

synonyms.from: sailing vessels stay close to land
depended on sea more than farming

synonyms.to: high-ranking members engaged in such activities

questions.text: Which TWO things did Colin find most satisfying about his bread reuse project?
questions.options: A: support from restaurants | B: preventing waste | C: overcoming problems | D: experimenting designs | E: learning 3D printing

questions.text: Which TWO things did Colin find most satisfying?
Which TWO ways could touch-sensitive sensors be developed in future?
questions.options: A: medical products | B: food fit to eat | C: drinks | D: blind people | E: indicate weight

questions.text: Which TWO ways could touch-sensitive sensors be developed in future?
Opinion about use of local products
Opinion about reduction in unnecessary packaging
Opinion about gluten-free and lactose-free food
Opinion about branded products related to celebrity chefs
Opinion about development of ghost kitchens
Opinion about use of mushrooms for common health concerns

errors.detail: 双选题。Colin 最满意的是防止浪费（B）和尝试图案颜色（D）。他表达满意度比较含蓄，B 是用 It was 回应别人的夸奖，D 是直接说 really pleased。
同一双选题，漏了第二个正确答案 D。
触感传感器未来方向是药品存储（A）和指示重量（E）。B 食物可吃是现有功能，D 助盲是原始设计目的，都不是未来发展。被当前用途干扰。
同一双选题，漏了第二个正确答案 E。
本地食材趋势。Colin 说 shops still full of imported foods + they need to be more proactive，答案是 D（零售商应更主动）。你选了 B，听到负面评价但没抓住主体是 retailers。
减少包装。Colin 明确说 The government needs to do something，所以是 G（应有更严法规）。你选了 D（零售商应更主动），把 Q25 的 retailer 串到了 Q26。
明星厨师品牌。Colin 说 bought a spice mix and it didn't actually taste of anything → 导致失望（B）。你选了 G（更严法规），原文没提法规。
蘑菇保健品。正确答案 H（可能有危险），你选了 E（需要更多资金支持）。可能听到 financial/support 相关词但配错对象。
原文说 weigh more than the average（更重），题目说 light（更轻），明确相反
混淆 FALSE 和 NOT GIVEN。原文提到 Werner Fischer 用了 spaghetti-strung 球拍，但没说是不是他设计的。没提供信息 = NOT GIVEN
同义替换不敏感。climatic conditions = weather 没识别出来
原文是复数 weights（对应 four to five），题目要求 ONE WORD ONLY from the passage，必须用原文原词

answers.text: People had expected Andy Murray to become the world's top tennis player for at least 5 years before 2016
The change that Andy Murray made to his rackets attracted a lot of attention
Most of the world's top players take a professional racket stringer on tour
Mike and Bob Bryan use rackets that are light in comparison to the majority
Werner Fischer played with a spaghetti-strung racket that he designed himself
The weather can affect how professional players adjust the strings on their rackets
It was believed that the change Pete Sampras made to his rackets contributed to his strong serve
Mike and Bob Bryan made changes to the types of ___ on their racket frames
Players were not allowed to use the spaghetti-strung racket because of amount of ___
Changes to rackets can be regarded as as important as players' diets or ___
All rackets used to have natural strings made from the ___ of animals
Pete Sampras had metal ___ put into the frames of his rackets
Gongalo Oliveira changed the ___ on his racket handles

synonyms.to: for at least five years before

synonyms.from: so subtle as to pass more or less unnoticed
weigh more than the average model
synonyms.to: light in comparison to the majority
had metal weights put into frames
changed the grips on racket handles

synonyms.source: weigh more than the average model

history.title: "task2"
history.topic: "Some people think that competition at work, at school and in daily life is a good thing. Others believe that we should try to cooperate more, rather than competing against each other."

errors.detail: 主谓不一致：Competition and Cooperation is → are
缺少系动词：whenever we in → whenever we are in
词性混淆：we should be competition → we should compete
主谓不一致：competition and cooperation is → are
主谓不一致：Temperate competition have → has
主谓不一致：which benefit → which benefits
冠词错误：a important → an important
搭配混淆：play a effect (应为 play a role / have an effect)
necessery → necessary
remarkble → remarkable
improvent → improvement
acknowledge → knowledge（形近词混淆）
clobrators → collaborators
incressing → increase
espicially → especially
studens → students
statue → state（形近词混淆）
spirit 用词不自然
contract to → compared to
fight 过于激烈
Temperate → Moderate
未覆盖 daily life 的例子
work → school 缺过渡句
enterprise → students 跳跃太快

rewriteHighlights.to: play a positive role / have a beneficial impact

questions.text: During the visit to Malatte, in France, members especially enjoyed
questions.options: A: theme park | B: river trip | C: cheese factory

questions.text: What will happen in Stanthorpe to mark the 25th anniversary?
questions.options: A: tree planted | B: garden seat | C: footbridge

questions.text: Which event raised most funds this year?
questions.options: A: film show | B: pancake evening | C: cookery demo

questions.text: First evening with French visitors: host families advised to
questions.options: A: walk round town | B: local restaurant | C: meal at home

questions.text: On Saturday evening there will be the chance to
questions.options: A: concert | B: match | C: competition

questions.text: Farm shop
Disabled entry
Adventure playground
Kitchen gardens
The Temple of the Four Winds

errors.detail: 发音 /striːm/，ea 组合长音，拼写误作 strin
被上下文提前出现的词干扰
没听到定位词后的答案词
被后方远距离词干扰
自己替换了原文词汇
价格数字不完整，漏了 .95
单选干扰选项
单选干扰选项
地图定位错误
地图定位错误
地图定位错误
地图定位错误

reviewHistory.source: 阅读同义替换 + 听力 Section 3 词汇

tasks.title: Matching Information 专项速度训练
tasks.time: 限时 15min（每道题最多 60 秒，到点跳）→ 10min 错因分析

tasks.title: 听力配对·主语追踪训练
tasks.task_type: Section 3 配对题专项（练谁说了什么）
tasks.time: 20min 做题 + 10min 对答案看原文

tasks.source: vocab 复习 + 明天新词
